title: Entandrophragma cylindricum
taxonomy: Entandrophragma cylindricum is classified as a species of tree in the family Meliaceae. It belongs to the genus Entandrophragma, which comprises several species of African trees. The genus Entandrophragma is further classified under the order Sapindales, and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification places Entandrophragma cylindricum within the broader kingdom Plantae. As a member of the Meliaceae family, Entandrophragma cylindricum is related to other trees commonly known as mahogany or African mahogany.
uses: Entandrophragma cylindricum, also known as the African mahogany or yew wood, has been found to have various medicinal uses in traditional African medicine. The bark and roots of the plant are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Extracts from the plant have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. Traditionally, the plant is also used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential benefits and applications of the plant's medicinal properties.
benefits: Entandrophragma cylindricum, also known as the African mahogany or yacouba tree, is a valuable species in traditional African medicine. The tree has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its bark and roots are used to make teas and infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. properties: Entandrophragma cylindricum, also known as the Nigerian mahogany, has been used in traditional medicine for its various therapeutic properties. The wood of this tree is reported to have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can aid in treating wounds and infections. In addition, extracts from the tree have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The tree's bark is also used in traditional medicine to treat fever and other ailments. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Entandrophragma cylindricum for therapeutic purposes.
constituents: Entandrophragma cylindricum, also known as the African mahogany or yew-wood, is a tree species native to tropical West Africa. Research on the active constituents of E. cylindricum is limited, but it is known to contain a variety of compounds, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids. These compounds are believed to contribute to the tree's medicinal properties and may have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Further studies are needed to fully understand the active constituents of E. cylindricum and their potential applications. The tree's heartwood is particularly valued for its durability and resistance to rot, decay, and insect damage.
parts: Entandrophragma cylindricum, commonly known as the yew wood, is a tree species native to tropical Africa. The bark of this tree is known to have medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. preparations: Entandrophragma cylindricum, commonly known as the African mahogany, is used in traditional medicine in various parts of Africa. The wood of the tree is valued for its medicinal properties, and herbal preparations are made from its bark, roots, and leaves. intro: Entandrophragma cylindricum, commonly known as the Sipo or African mahogany, is a deciduous tree species native to tropical Africa. The Sipo tree has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Entandrophragma cylindricum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sipo include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
